name: Chinese: Shrimp in Chinese Lobster Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 Quarter-sized slice fresh ginger, peeled and minced
1 tbsp Shao Hsing rice wine or dry sherry
2 tsp Cornstarch blended with 1 tablespoon water
recipeInstructions:
1. Shell and de-vein the shrimp. Butterfly the shrimp by slicing them down the length of the back, stopping just above the tail. Rinse with cold water and blot dry.
2. Cover the black beans with lukewarm water; let soak for 5 minutes. Drain.
3. Combine the black beans with the minced garlic and ginger, gently crushing into a paste. Mix in the wine; set aside.
4. Place a wok over medium-high heat. When hot, drizzle in half of the oil. Add the shrimp and stir-fry until they begin to curl and turn bright orange, about 1 minute. Remove to a dish and keep warm.
5. Reheat the wok over medium heat; add the remaining tablespoon of oil and salt. Add the black bean paste and sauté for a few seconds until it becomes aromatic.
6. Increase the heat to medium-high. Add the pork and stir-fry until the morsels are no longer pink, about 3 minutes.
7. Add onions, peppers, sugar, white pepper, and soy sauce; toss together until the vegetables begin to soften, about 1 minute. Add the stock and keep tossing until it comes to a boil.
8. Stir the cornstarch mixture to recombine, then drizzle into the center of the wok, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens.
